narrative.headline: 2020 Honda CR-V: collision-avoidance and air bag complaints lead the list
narrative.summary: Owners filed 324 complaints with NHTSA. The largest cluster is the forward-collision/automatic-braking system at 72 reports (22%), followed by air bags at 45 (14%) and the electrical system at 41 (13%). The fuel and engine systems round out the top five at 33 and 31 reports (10% each). Air bags drew the most crashes and injuries (8 each), and two electrical-system reports involved a fire. Watch for the collision system failing or activating on its own, sudden air bag deployment, and gasoline mixing into the engine oil.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: The most-reported area: 72 complaints (22%). Owners describe the collision-mitigation system failing to work or no longer functioning. Linked to 4 crashes and 2 injuries.

issues.sample: TL* THE CONTACT OWNS A 2020 HONDA CR-V. THE CONTACT STATED THAT HIS WIFE WAS ATTEMPTING TO MAKE A LEFT TURN FROM A STOP LIGHT, THE DRIVER'S AIR BAG FROM THE STEERING WHEEL SUDDENLY DEPLOYED RIGHT IN THE MIDDLE OF HER MAKING THE LEFT TURN. THE CONTACT'S WIFE WAS STUNNED AND IMPAIR
issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: 45 complaints (14%), with the most crashes and injuries of any area (8 each). Owners report the air bag deploying suddenly during normal driving.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 41 complaints (13%). Owners report the instrument display going dark while driving and warning lights cycling on. Linked to 2 fires, 3 crashes, and 3 injuries.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 33 complaints (10%), including reports of fuel leaks. Linked to 1 injury, with no crashes or fires reported in this group.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 31 complaints (10%). A common theme is gasoline diluting the engine oil. No crashes, fires, or injuries reported in this group.
